items.why_it_matters: A user who names a service direction is much easier to route into a useful next step than someone who only says they want a change.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the direction and keep gathering timing plus current-hair facts.

items.why_it_matters: Timing is one of the clearest signs that a salon inquiry is moving toward action.
items.echoer_action: Keep the reply warm and direct the next step toward booking facts.

items.why_it_matters: Current hair condition changes appointment scope and routing, so it is an important conversion detail rather than a side note.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge the detail and keep narrowing time plus service.

items.why_it_matters: First-time uncertainty means the reply should optimize for clarity and low pressure, not upsell speed.
items.echoer_action: Use calm language and guide toward a simple service filter.

items.why_it_matters: Event-driven hair requests often convert quickly if slot and service fit are handled well.
items.echoer_action: Acknowledge urgency and ask for event date plus available slot.

items.why_it_matters: When the concern is personal and exposed in public, the response must protect dignity while staying useful.
items.echoer_action: Reply gently, avoid echoing the shame language, and guide toward a safer next step.
